title: plagidia riparia
side_effects: Plagidia riparia can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to eye irritation, blurred vision, and in severe cases, permanent damage to the cornea. Misuse of the plant can also result in cardiovascular issues, such as rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations.
